title: Ophiopogon hayatae
intro: Ophiopogon hayatae, also known as Japanese Dwarf Mondo Grass or Ha-taya Grass, is a type of flowering plant in the Asparagaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ophiopogon hayatae include iridoids, saponins, and other phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Ophiopogon hayatae is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional Asian remedies such as the Japanese herbal medicine, "Kakkon-to".
taxonomy: Ophiopogon hayatae is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It is native to Taiwan and is a type of grass-like perennial with strap-shaped leaves. The plant is commonly known as the 'Taiwan Fountain Grass' due to its ornamental value. Ophiopogon hayatae is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens and is valued for its attractive foliage and delicate white flowers. The species is considered a rare and endangered plant due to habitat loss and overcollection.
